reviews.text: I spent weeks stressed out trying to figure out where to turn, and a friend told me about this office. They were super knowledgeable and took the time to help me understand what was happening with my worker’s compensation claim. Everyone was incredibly responsive and the outcome ended up being a great settlement that honestly lifted a weight off my shoulders.

I had a great experience with Goldberg & Loren, and I want to especially thank Carmen Picazo, along with my attorneys Alfredo and Raul, for helping me successfully settle my case. From start to finish, the entire team was professional, responsive, and truly supportive.

Carmen was incredibly knowledgeable and patient, always taking the time to explain every step and keep me informed. Alfredo and Raul were outstanding attorneys—professional, thorough, and clearly dedicated to achieving the best possible outcome for me. Together, they made what could have been a very stressful process feel smooth and reassuring.

I’m very grateful for the care and attention I received and highly recommend Goldberg & Loren to anyone looking for a reliable and effective legal team.

Jose did an excellent job handling my case from start to finish. He was professional, responsive, and took the time to explain everything clearly so I always knew what was going on. The process went smoothly, and I’m very satisfied with the outcome. I highly recommend Jose to anyone needing a knowledgeable and reliable attorney.

reviews.text:
I hired Mr. Whittington's office to represent me for a traffic ticket I received while traveling that I could not appear for myself. Initially his office was very friendly, he spent several minutes on the phone discussing my situation and they were consistent in following up with me to confirm if I would like to retain them and for my $600 payment.

After paying and securing the representation, there was minimal communication as my case was postponed several times. After the actual hearing, I did receive a lengthy email outlining what was done and the results of the case. They were about what I expected to happen. However it did leave me with several questions about making the fine payment and traffic school. I sent emails and have left messages since December 10th. Given it was the holidays I was patient in expecting a reply. But it is now January 25th and I still haven't received a response.

While the results were what I expected, the cost of the representation was about the cost of the initial fine. Given that I had paid so much, I don't feel it is expecting too much to receive guidance following the results of the hearing.

This is not a common scenario for me, so I don't know if this is standard practice for an attorney in this situation. But as a consumer, I am disappointed with the lack of client service and professionalism after the payment was received. I just expected more for $600.
reviews.ownerResponse: We’re are very sorry that you’re experience was disappointing and would make it up to you on your next citation.

reviews.text:
Represented! Joe got done all I could as for in my case. Bottom line, I was deceptively charged for going  a speed which was over the posted limit. Joe defended me in court and ensured that the truth prevailed. In a day and age where truth and integrity is so hard to come by, he enhanced my faith in the justice system proving that there are still times when truth wins over lies!
His contact was consistent and was always available for my questions and answered them in an understandable way. Following my case (which I did not personally attend) he emailed me a full description of the directives made by the court in my case and provided an explanation for how it came about. I appreciate Joe and his team in representing me to what I felt was the fullest of their abilities. I encourage others to partner with them if the circumstance arises needing defense.
There's certain people you would rather not see very often because it typically means life isn't going as planned (doctors,police officers, attorneys) but, if I do somehow need defense in traffic court; Joe is my guy!! Cheers to truth & success!

reviews.text: This firm has now sent a BK notice to my credit card companies and my mortage company. Only catch I am not filing BK. They claim they are not doing this but yet I now have 3 creditors suspending my accounts because a BK filed by this attorney. I call and they claim they are not sending out any notices; yet now it's 3 claiming they are so... Clients beware seems like they have some pretty bad case managment or paperwork. UGHH
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi. You apparently live in Arizona. I am not licensed to practice in Arizona and do not have any clients in Arizona-you’re not my client. You have the same name as a client, but you are not him. Bankruptcy attorneys do not send out notices to the creditors-the bankruptcy court does. Clearly, your mortgage company and the 2 creditors you mentioned have you confused with our client.

I understand you are frustrated, but your problem is with your creditors (and potentially credit reporting agencies), not me. You should speak to those creditors to confirm your social security number (I’m sure it’s different than my client’s), and have them straighten out their mistake. You clearly do not know how bankruptcy noticing works, and are taking it out on my office. You should get your facts straight before you post misguided and completely wrong information.

He is a great lawyer he actually goes above and beyond and cares about his client.

I had a domestic violence (DV) case in 2016. During the course of my case, my attorney–client privilege was violated. Mr. Salas instructed me to communicate a message to my ex-partner so that she could prepare a letter attesting to my good moral character.

Mr. Salas’s associates, including Antonio Lopez, failed to communicate effectively with one another regarding a letter that my ex-partner delivered to Mr. Salas’s office. After I was released following six months of incarceration and reviewed my case—including the back-and-forth exchange of information between defense counsel and the District Attorney’s Office—I discovered that Antonio Lopez-Hutchinson had emailed that letter to the District Attorney’s Office.

This was the same letter my ex-partner had provided to Mr. Salas, in which she admitted that she had lied and that she was not at home when the alleged events occurred. In the letter, my ex-partner attempted to amend and recant her prior statements in order to clear my name, asserting that I was innocent, that I was not responsible for her injuries, and that a third party was involved.

She further explained that she had falsely accused me because I had contacted her lover’s wife, which caused him to become angry with her. She stated that she was assaulted by that individual but nonetheless accused me of indirectly causing her harm.

On my first day at Lerdo, I nearly lost my life after being assaulted by other inmates. I was transported to Kern Medical Center (KMC) by patrol car. I was struck with a commercial broomstick and immediately lost consciousness. I was stomped, kicked, and punched, resulting in a fracture to the front-left portion of my cranium, a fractured jaw, a broken nose, lacerations to both eyebrows, facial disfigurement, dislocated fingers, and extensive bruising throughout my body.

My mother contacted Mr. Salas’s office, but he never came to see me. Instead, it was my ex-wife’s attorneys who visited me in jail and assisted me with the necessary medical procedures. I paid Mr. Salas over $30,000 for what I experienced as extremely poor representation and a deeply negative experience.

At the time, I was also suffering from a work-related injury sustained in 2013 and had an active workers’ compensation case. I had significant difficulty walking and moving and was in a very poor emotional and mental state. Despite this, Mr. Salas had the audacity to ask me how much my workers’ compensation case was worth, which I found inappropriate. I did not disclose that information to him.

Attorney Antonio Lopez didn't do anything but he, judge and the district attorney went in the back to talk. reviews.text: I would like to start off by saying what an amazing law firm The Accident Guys has been with my case. My vehicle was rear ended by a negligent driver causing both damage to my vehicle and my body. The back of my car got smashed in and I could not open the trunk door. I ended up getting treatment for my severe whiplash. The accident was a stressful experience but the team at The Accident Guys reassured me that everything would be ok. First, I had a meeting with a nice representative, Carlo who handled the initial steps of my case. Then I was assigned to Case Manager Charry. Charry was such a sincere and kind case manager that I got to work with. She was attentive when handling details of my case. She was excellent at explaining the case process and informed me with progress updates along the way. In addition, Charry referred me to great medical and chiropractic facilities for my body recovery treatments.  Secondly, I would like to praise Attorney Phillip who worked very hard with his legal team on my case. Attorney Philip was very kind, understanding and sympathetic about what I experienced with my accident. He really made my case a positive experience when I felt down about it. He also offered good advice and suggestions to move my case forward and was able to get me a settlement beyond what I expected. Lastly, I was given a small gift card for my birthday which was the sweetest gesture. I am grateful to have made the right decision to have The Accident Guys represent me.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Rose, thank you so much for taking the time to share your experience in such detail—it means a lot to us. We’re grateful you chose us to represent you and we wish you continued healing and all the best moving forward!

reviews.text: The Accident Guys turned a potentially overwhelming ordeal into a manageable experience! Recently my husband and I had an estate plan drawn up by Stevie Jo McDonald at Borton Petrini and we were very pleased with our experience.
Dealing with what happens if we are no longer able to care for ourselves or what happens after we pass away is a difficult, uncomfortable and stressful process to go through, Stevie made it so much easier.
Stevie was not only professional but also uncommonly kind, accommodating our needs by conducting our business by phone. She gathered all the information she needed to prepare the plan and answered all our questions.
After all the paperwork was done and sent to us she once again had a phone conference with us and went over every page with us making sure we understood everything and answering all our questions.
Stevie taught us so much about how to preserve  and protect our estate for ourselves and our children and their futures.
We would highly recommend Borton Petrini for your estate planning needs.

I would like to thank Diana and her team for thee exceptional service in handling my case. They were very professional in keeping me up to date every step of the way in my case. As an out of state resident, if they had any quick questions or needed anything, they were quick to notify me via email or by phone. I would definitely recommend them to anyone who needs a lawyer in Bakersfield California.

I had a domestic violence (DV) case in 2016. During the course of my case, my attorney–client privilege was violated. Mr. Salas instructed me to communicate a message to my ex-partner so that she could prepare a letter attesting to my good moral character.

Mr. Salas’s associates, including Antonio Lopez, failed to communicate effectively with one another regarding a letter that my ex-partner delivered to Mr. Salas’s office. After I was released following six months of incarceration and reviewed my case—including the back-and-forth exchange of information between defense counsel and the District Attorney’s Office—I discovered that Antonio Lopez-Hutchinson had emailed that letter to the District Attorney’s Office.

This was the same letter my ex-partner had provided to Mr. Salas, in which she admitted that she had lied and that she was not at home when the alleged events occurred. In the letter, my ex-partner attempted to amend and recant her prior statements in order to clear my name, asserting that I was innocent, that I was not responsible for her injuries, and that a third party was involved.

She further explained that she had falsely accused me because I had contacted her lover’s wife, which caused him to become angry with her. She stated that she was assaulted by that individual but nonetheless accused me of indirectly causing her harm.

On my first day at Lerdo, I nearly lost my life after being assaulted by other inmates. I was transported to Kern Medical Center (KMC) by patrol car. I was struck with a commercial broomstick and immediately lost consciousness. I was stomped, kicked, and punched, resulting in a fracture to the front-left portion of my cranium, a fractured jaw, a broken nose, lacerations to both eyebrows, facial disfigurement, dislocated fingers, and extensive bruising throughout my body.

My mother contacted Mr. Salas’s office, but he never came to see me. Instead, it was my ex-wife’s attorneys who visited me in jail and assisted me with the necessary medical procedures. I paid Mr. Salas over $30,000 for what I experienced as extremely poor representation and a deeply negative experience.

At the time, I was also suffering from a work-related injury sustained in 2013 and had an active workers’ compensation case. I had significant difficulty walking and moving and was in a very poor emotional and mental state. Despite this, Mr. Salas had the audacity to ask me how much my workers’ compensation case was worth, which I found inappropriate. I did not disclose that information to him.

My ex recanted the day of my sentence infront of the judge. I noticed that the judge got really mad/upset and send one of the guards to look for my ex-wife attorney.  She told her off the record that he was going to pretend he didn't heard or otherwise she was going to jail.  Attorney Antonio Lopez didn't do anything but he, judge and the district attorney went in the back to talk.  Attorney Lopez only said to me that there was nothing to do since I had signed a plea deal.

I absolutely enjoyed my independence as a Lyft driver (proud 5-star driver). My last ride ended in a tragic, absolutely avoidable accident. I know I would need legal representation to get me through this rough, frightening, and overwhelming time. I wanted to be represented by the best, a beast a of firm. I did my research and read countless reviews, I consulted with family and friends; I finally decided to have West Coast Trial Lawyers in Los Angeles represent me. I am so relieved I did this.

Everyone there treated me like an A+ client. Every single question was answered with patience and integrity. Mike made sure I received top notch medical treatment; I was in so much pain. The woman that I hit did not work with her insurance company let alone with my team of excellent Lawyers. She ruined my life, my kids life, and the life of the foster kids I cared for.

If you are looking for legal representation that will fight tooth and nail for you, look no further than WCTL. If you want a lawyer that ensures his/her mission is to see you smile again, choose WCTL. If you want a lawyer that cares, call WCTL.

I have no regrets choosing WCTL as my legal representation, neither will you! Thank you Mike.

The notary fee turned out to be $100 more than we were initially told, but that isn't on her but moreso on the notary. We are very much at ease now knowing that we have our trust, will, advanced health care directives and the guardianship for our children under wraps. If you're in need of someone that genuinely cares, Stevie Jo is your person.

Finally he sent the 60 days notice too late and on top of it, it was incorrect, after a long discussion he finally sent the notice with the right reasoning. When J.J. Dominguez founded The Dominguez Firm more than 30 years ago, his goal was to provide the community with quality legal representation. While working in trauma centers across Los Angeles hospitals, he saw firsthand how insurance companies refused to fairly compensate injured accident victims.

Today, The Dominguez Firm is one of the most recognized and successful personal injury law firms in California. We are not a referral service, nor will we settle your accident injury claim for less just to close it. We have the resources to fight for the full compensation you're entitled to.

Thank you for entrusting your case with us. We wish you all the best!

reviews.text:
Victor Nasser is an absolute force and hands down the best lawyer we could have ever asked for. When everything was on the line, he stepped in and fought like it was his own family at stake. Thanks to his skill, determination, and refusal to back down, every single felony charge against me was completely dismissed. That outcome changed our lives forever.

And Mr. Nasser didn’t stop there. He went even further and made sure my property was returned from the Kern County Sheriff’s Department — something most attorneys wouldn’t even bother with once a case is over. That level of commitment is rare and speaks volumes about who he is as both a lawyer and a person.

He is sharp, fearless, honest, and relentless. He communicates clearly, stays ten steps ahead, and makes you feel protected during the most stressful moments imaginable. He gave us confidence when we were scared, clarity when things felt uncertain, and results when it mattered most.

Because of everything he did for us, Victor Nasser will always be our family’s lawyer. There is no one else we would trust with our freedom or our future. If you want an attorney who will go to war for you and never stop fighting, Victor Nasser is the one.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Highly Recommended Attorney

I want to give a huge thank you to my attorney for going above and beyond on my cases. Not only did he help me beat a misdemeanor and get a felony dismissed in the interest of justice, but he didn’t stop there. He followed through with a motion to get all of my seized property returned, and even took the extra step to help seal and destroy my record so I can move forward with my life without that weight on my shoulders.

His professionalism, communication, and dedication were on another level. He fought for me every step of the way and truly cared about the outcome. If you need someone who will actually show up for you, defend you, and get results, this is the attorney you want in your corner.

Highly recommend.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the kind words. We are proud to have helped in getting the “Not Guilty” verdict in your misdemeanor trial, winning the motion to suppress, and ultimately the dismissal of your felony case. We’re also glad we were able to follow through in getting your property back, and moving forward with sealing and destroying your record so you can move ahead without that burden on your shoulders. We wish you all the best, and we’re always here if you need anything in the future.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
The Attorney You Want in Your Corner – Victor Nasser is Truly One of a Kind

If I could give more than 5 stars, I would. From the very first conversation with Victor Nasser, I knew I was in the hands of someone who genuinely cares—not just about the case, but about me as a person. He didn’t treat me like a file number or just another client. Victor stood by my side like a trusted friend, a protective older brother, and an unwavering advocate. The way he represented me, it felt like he was fighting for his own family.

Victor is sharp, strategic, and deeply knowledgeable about the law—but what truly sets him apart is his heart. He listens with patience, explains with clarity, and moves with conviction. Throughout every stage of my case, he made sure I felt seen, heard, and supported. Even when things got tough or uncertain, Victor never wavered. He was always two steps ahead—calm, prepared, and focused on getting the best possible outcome for me.

There’s a rare kind of comfort that comes from knowing your attorney is not only a skilled professional but also someone who truly has your back. That’s exactly who Victor is. He brings integrity, compassion, and excellence to every single thing he does. I can honestly say I walked away not just with a legal victory, but with immense gratitude for having crossed paths with someone like him.

If you’re looking for an attorney who will treat your case with the same dedication and intensity as if it were his own family’s, look no further. Victor Nasser is the attorney you want in your corner. He is, without question, absolutely outstanding.

Thank you, Victor. You changed my life.

The Best Lawyer in California!

Francis Henríquez
I want to express my deepest gratitude to attorney Francis Henríquez for helping me obtain my permanent residency. My case wasn't easy; on the contrary, it was quite complicated. However, she worked with extraordinary dedication, patience, and professionalism. She always explained each step to me, fought for me, and never lost faith that we would succeed.

Today, with great joy, I have my permanent residency card in my hands. Thanks to her advice and guidance, I know how to use it responsibly and appreciate this great opportunity.

Next week, I will travel to Mexico after 23 years without seeing my mother. Just imagining that moment fills me with emotion: seeing her, hugging her, going together to visit the Virgin of Guadalupe, and bringing a serenade and dinner for my whole family. All of this is possible thanks to the work and commitment of attorney Francis Henríquez.
I recommend her with complete confidence. I will always recommend her to my family, friends, and anyone who needs a capable, honest, and prepared lawyer to handle difficult cases. I am proof that she can make it happen. Thank you so much, Attorney Francis Henríquez. May God always bless you and your family. You helped me fulfill one of the biggest dreams of my life, and for that I will be eternally grateful.

I 100% recommend her!

Excellent team! (everyone)
– Agustín Isidoro

We are beyond grateful for all the work the Office of Francis Henriquez has done for us. They were recommended to us by a relative back in 2021. What we personally loved about the process is that they gave us a course plan with different options on our first consult.
Jocelyn Camarena was our paralegal, she was always one call, email or text away answering all of our questions at every step of the process. Especially the weeks leading to the interview, when she helped through the last-minute details of immigration filing case and the preparation for our interview. Jocelyn’s support gave us so much confidence, and I truly felt that she was invested in our success. They truly had the best team, if you are in the Los Angeles or Bakersfield, I highly recommend giving them a call and getting a consultation.

Extremely and forever grateful to Attorney Francis Henriquez. She was able to help us obtain my wife's permanent residency in the United States in a matter of months after the initial application. Her highly trained staff and extensive knowledge of immigration law were key to turning a potentially difficult case into the greatest blessing of our lives.

Attorney Francis Henriquez helped us from the first day we contacted her. She is a true professional and, most importantly, a kind and compassionate person. She helped us with monthly payments, which made the process much easier. Don't be fooled by notaries or tax preparers; the laws change frequently, and only an experienced attorney can help you. We will always be grateful to our attorney, Francis Henriquez.

Attorney Francis Henriquez

Thank you for helping me obtain my permanent residency.

Brandon quoted my Brother-in -law 1700$ for the work and then conveniently forgot he agreed on that. I offered to pay his price to add the documents for power of attorney, medical directive and deed update. Which now would cost 2500$, He agreed and stated it would take a week.I paid the whole amount upfront. We waited 2 months without a word. After multiple contacts the work was complete only lacking the updated deed. Which he did not complete and now my brother-in -law has to do himself. The papers were just handed over straight from copy and not even in a business folder or binder like a professional office would provide.

reviews.text: My wife and I chose this attorney to help us with our estate planning and he asked to see our old will/trust documents which had been previously done by another attorney but had since retired.  Our mistake was not only in hiring Mr. Brandon Martin, but another mistake was paying the full amount up front.  Our original estate planning attorney had completed our documents in two weeks.  Mr. Martin slow played us taking almost 6 months.  I wrote him several texts and email messages but to no avail.  At one point, he wrote back saying, "as you can see, I'm in no hurry to get this done.  Finally, on December 1, 2023, almost 6 months later, he contacted me saying our paperwork was ready to be signed.  When we went in to get our new Estate/trust documents, I asked him for our old original documents.  He said he didn't have them with him but he would mail them to us.  On January 2, 2024, he wrote me saying he had been in San Diego and mail us the documents the next day.  I offered to go pick them up.  Today is January 23, 2024 and we have not received our old documents.  Mr. Martin leaves me no choice but to assume this is personal and I don't know what he has against me or my wife, but there's no other explanation.  If you want to avoid problems, look elsewhere for a more professional attorney.  I don't recommend this man.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I note from the history of Tony's reviews of other businesses that he gives either five-star or one-star reviews, but rarely if ever anything in between. In this case, Tony received a full estate plan at a significantly discounted price as promised. Due to a full calendar on my end, changes in intent, and occasional unavailability on his end due to travel mostly, it took longer than usual to conduct the final signing. I took longer than usual to return originals of an old revoked estate plan because, as an original, it was kept in a more secure storage area off-site.

I'd accept a 4-star review, which would still bring down my average here, because of the delay in delivering the old, defunct originals. There's no doubt that our office took longer than usual in responding to such a request. But, on a happy note, this was not personal and I doubt the other outstanding local businesses who have received one star reviews were acting maliciously, either. I think Tony is a fascinating person and I enjoyed talking with him in our meetings very much.

This is long, but a worthy read, if you don't have time  bottom line, DO NOT hire this firm!  First little background on how workers compensation works so this really makes sense. When you're injured it is intimidating and often times scary to deal with. However, when all is said and done, the state has a chart that lines up your body part(s) injured and the % of permanent disability of that body part. Put the two together and you have the amount of money it's "worth"... Nothing more. Lawyers manipulate the situation and make themselves seem NEEDED. The fact of the matter is, they can't get you more money than what the precalculated amount. When  you do get more, it's because they find additional injuries and aliments to add to your claim, usually holding up treatment for whatever you were originally in this situation for to begin with. This also ups their percentage and THEY get more money. So, my story: my late husband was injured at work in 2011. We dealt with workers compensation directly for a while and he wasn't satisfied with the speed of his treatment. This is very standard, lawyer or not.  Unfortunately it's hard to know how this really works until you're already in it. So, he hired what used to be The Law office of Wm. Todd Berry, now Berry Smith and Bartell. After his consultation and signing a contract they said he needed to be switched to THEIR doctor,  Alan Moelleken, this was another joke, but I'll spare the details. However, a law office has absolutely no business giving medical advice and telling you which Dr to see. The fact is, an attorney gets in cahoots with the Drs office and they can work together to manipulate the situation. My late husband started with a spine and hip injury. After the lawyer getting involved and being switched to THEIR doctor, he had hip, spine, knee, psychological damage, and "injuries to unspecified body parts." So, fast forward,  with all these  aliments and lack of treatment he ends up on disability, loses his job over it, and all they're doing is throwing copius amounts of pain medication at him. He ends up passing away after his respiratory drive was knocked out from all combination of medication he was on. He passed away in July 2013. Attorney client relationship ends at death. Berry Smith and Bartell no longer had a client. I did supply them with a death certificate which should have been supplied to the court and thus terminating any involvement from them. I never reached out to them after that, I never signed a contract or fee waiver. I was NOT their client.  However, they never reported the death to the court and never removed themselves from the case. I do wish I had a better idea of how all this worked so I could have been more aware of the crap they were pulling, but I was learning as I went. I had no desire to deal with them and started working directly with State Fund to resolve the issue. AFTER we reached a settlement, Berry Smith and Bartell, swooped in and promptly put a lien on the death settlement. The battle with them held up the money that was intended to care for my children, who lost their father for almost 5 years. The stress and emotional trauma from fighting them has caused our family more grief than you could imagine. Andrea Lopez from BSB is the lawer I had the "pleasure" of dealing with in court. The first jusge we had strongly encouraged her to drop it. She all but had a temper tantrum in the court room. She's rude and manipulative. Finally, after years of fighting, winning at trial, the court has denied their appeal (because they just would NOT drop it). Something that could have been complete in 2014, and would have let my grieving family better move on was drug out until January 2019. They'd do anything to to keep their greedy hands in the pot. They are unethical, manipulative, greedy, and I can only hope my story will keep others from being deceived and ever letting them get involved to begin with.

reviews.text: When a 10 year old felony arrest warrant surprisingly appeared on a live scan background check, fear immediately crept in. I was referred by a friend to John Underwood Law. From the start they released me of my fear and walked me through how my case was going to be approached. Every step that John laid out was executed as he explained. He said his goal was to get complete dismissal. I was not so sure this promise would be kept being that I had indeed confessed to the felony crime. But he was true to his word. I didn’t even have to show up to court. My case was completely dismissed. Nothing on my record. I also want to add that I have been arrested in the past so him promising dismissal for a convicted felon was not realistic from my perspective. But HE DID IT!!! I’m forever grateful to Underwood Law. Although I hope to never need legal representation ever again, but should the need arise, John will be the only person I will ever call!! Or recommend to anyone in need.

A detailed billing showing date, time spent, and amount billed is always provided as required by law.  Once, your bankruptcy case is dismissed, the creditors can call you and want to get paid.  The creditors may be persistent and not nice. So, if you’d like to file a chapter 13 Bankruptcy—not make the payments as required by the court order confirming the plan—your case is going to get dismissed.  No attorney can lower the amounts required to be paid to keep your house, cars and other assets.

reviews.text:
Bankruptcy in Bakersfield can be a stressful and overwhelming experience for individuals and businesses alike. It is a legal process that provides individuals and businesses with the opportunity to eliminate or restructure their debts when they are unable to meet their financial obligations. In Bakersfield, bankruptcy can provide a fresh start for those struggling with overwhelming debt, but it is important to understand the process and seek the guidance of a qualified bankruptcy attorney.

There are various types of bankruptcy filings available in Bakersfield, including Chapter 7 and Chapter 13. Chapter 7 bankruptcy, also known as liquidation bankruptcy, involves the sale of a debtor's non-exempt assets to pay off creditors. On the other hand, Chapter 13 bankruptcy, also known as reorganization bankruptcy, allows individuals to restructure their debts and create a repayment plan over a period of three to five years. Each type of bankruptcy has its own set of requirements and implications, so it is crucial to consult with a knowledgeable bankruptcy attorney to determine the best course of action for your specific financial situation.

The process of filing for bankruptcy in Bakersfield can be complex and time-consuming. From gathering financial documentation to attending mandatory credit counseling sessions, there are numerous steps involved in a bankruptcy filing. Additionally, the bankruptcy court in Bakersfield requires a thorough and accurate disclosure of one's financial situation, including income, expenses, assets, and liabilities. Therefore, it is highly recommended to seek the assistance of a reputable bankruptcy attorney who has experience navigating the intricacies of the bankruptcy process in Bakersfield.

It is important to note that bankruptcy in Bakersfield is governed by federal laws, but there are also local rules and procedures that must be followed. For example, individuals filing for bankruptcy in Bakersfield must adhere to the specific requirements of the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Eastern District of California. This includes attending a meeting of creditors and fulfilling any additional obligations as stipulated by the court. Therefore, having a bankruptcy attorney who is familiar with the local bankruptcy laws and procedures can be invaluable in ensuring a smooth and successful bankruptcy filing.

Bankruptcy in Bakersfield can provide individuals and businesses with the chance to regain control of their financial lives and start anew. However, it is crucial to approach the process with careful consideration and comprehensive legal guidance. A knowledgeable bankruptcy attorney in Bakersfield can provide invaluable support and counsel throughout the entire bankruptcy process, from the initial consultation to the final discharge of debts.

In conclusion, bankruptcy in Bakersfield is a viable option for those facing overwhelming debt, but it is not a decision to be made lightly. It is essential to seek the guidance of a qualified bankruptcy attorney who can provide the necessary expertise and support throughout the bankruptcy process. By working with a knowledgeable attorney, individuals and businesses can navigate the complexities of bankruptcy with confidence and maximize their chances of achieving a fresh financial start.

reviews.text: My wife and I were pleased with the professional yet personal attention we received in working with Chris Botti.  I attended their seminar several years ago to learn more about estate planning.  Although I put off for a long time following up with scheduling an appointment to work on our estate plan, they never forgot to send regular reminders.  In hindsight, had I taken advantage of their policy to never charge for advice, which ironically was one of the things that appealed to me about their firm, I would have realized that some of my questions that had caused me to procrastinate were not as big of obstacles as I had thought, and we would have been able to sleep easier much earlier by putting some fairly easy safeguards in place for our children.  Because I had decided to pay a deposit at the time of the seminar, there was no question about the fee for the service being charged at the original lower rate offered years ago.  But even setting that aside, although there are do-it-yourself options to creating trusts and wills, we had decided that guaranteeing our wishes with respect to our loved ones after we're gone was important enough to go to an expert to make sure it is done right.  Mr. Botti has decades of experience, and it was evident in the documents that he helped us put in place and answers to our questions that our estate plan reflected up-to-date legal considerations and was comprehensive in covering all scenarios that we deemed important.  We are satisfied that we made the right decision to go with Botti & Morison.

reviews.text:
Just over a year ago, I got the grizzly news that I had developed Parkinson’s disease, a debilitating, neurological disease with no known cure.

I grew up in Hawaii in the middle of a large industrial farm (10K+ acres) and golf course community in the 60s and 70s that frequently used the chemical for weed control just a few feet from my home’s back door. The prevailing trade winds kept these toxic fumes swirling in and out of our well ventilated home.

After doing my research, I signed on as a plaintiff with Natick & Cohen, and I’m glad I did. My representative, Zana, energetically took my statement of facts and circumstances, transcribed and documented my historical recollections and accounts of how I was most likely exposed to this deadly and toxic chemical compound.

I have easily been able to submit further materials to Zana, like aerial photos and maps, showing my immediate vicinity to where paraquat was known to be used. I am extremely confident I have chosen the right firm, given the excellent service and responsiveness of my legal representative at Natick and Cohen. I look forward to the results of that engagement with Zana and her team.

The debilitation I’m currently suffering is frightening & will only get worse, and I do need a law firm that will fight for me and I feel I’ve made a good choice. Godspeed to Zana and her team.
reviews.ownerResponse: Bart, we appreciate your taking the time to share a detailed review of your experience with our law firm. We really appreciate it!

reviews.text:

Just wanted you to know that you have been doing a wonderful job on our case.
Thank you so much.
Mr. And Mrs Janes.

reviews.text: My experience with Larry H Parker has been amazing. From the beginning I had Chris who worked very closely to ensure that I got all my information to them and the process was started very quickly. During the car accident I was pregnant with a Surrogate baby and he made sure all my needs were met. Once delivering Chris made sure I got my MRI and started the chiropractor as soon as possible because I hurt my back badly in the accident. I then went to physical therapy and Chris made sure each and every appointment was scheduled and even sent me reminders. Since the case had a lot to collect Chris let me know if I needed a loan that they work with companies since I lost my job from fracturing my wrist. He then got the loan pushed through that day. He was truly a life saver for me!! Then once everything was processed and inputted Joe reached out with the insurance claim amount once I was fine with settling he also let me know I was able to take out another small loan because I was falling behind on my rent and car payment. Joe has been sending me updates and always gets back to me as soon as possible. Overall I would definitely recommend Larry H Parker because I didn’t even think I had a case to begin with and they have given me so much opportunities when it came to my case!! Thank you Larry H Parker for being there for me and my children throughout this whole process.

My father is an old fashioned man (83 years old) who does not want to give out his credit card information or any other his financial information before he actually receives his service. when he visited the lawyer, the reception wanted to take his credit card information even before he sees the lawyer. So he walked away and came back home without seeing the lawyer. Next day this is the email that I got from the lawyer...

As you probably know by now, your father left as he declined to pay for the time he wanted me to spend with him.  I just cannot imagine why he would think my time is free, when my time is the commodity I have to sell.  I do not know of any real estate attorneys who provide free consultations, so there is no one I know to refer you to.  New attorneys may provide free consultations, but I have practiced real estate law for 40 years, and I just don't need to do that as I am busy enough as it is.

I sincerely hope your father finds an attorney who he respects enough not to quibble about paying.  We work very hard to provide quality service for a fair price.

Good luck!

Fawn Kennedy Dessy

My recent experience with this firm compels me to share a public account, to ensure transparency and prevent others from encountering similar unprofessional conduct.

I approached this firm seeking legal assistance regarding a matter of substantial value, supported by concrete and compelling evidence. Rather than providing representation capable of advocating for me in court, they explicitly refused to do so, offering only a demand letter as their “assistance.” This refusal, despite the gravity and clear strength of the case, is inconsistent with the expectations of any competent legal service.

In addition to this minimal effort, members of the firm pressured me to provide positive reviews in exchange for their limited services. This coercive quid pro quo behavior is wholly inappropriate and ethically indefensible.

The demand letter produced was inadequate and substandard, failing to reflect the seriousness of the matter or any strategic understanding of the case. It was, in short, not helpful and did not advance my case in any meaningful way.

Moreover, staff members asked for my home address and referenced members of my family whom I never disclosed, which was deeply concerning. All interactions have been thoroughly documented.

Given this pattern of refusal to provide competent representation, coercion, and irregular behavior, I terminated all contact with the firm.

I share this account so the public may be aware and exercise caution when engaging with this organization. No client should be subjected to coercion or inadequate service when seeking legal support.

reviews.text: My mother scheduled an appointment for her parents both of which are hard of hearing. They were referred by a friend who had good things to say about Lary Wright. However, when they walked in to meet Bill, he did not stand up and introduce himself, in fact he didn’t stand up a single time. My mother reiterated several times that her parents could not hear him with the mask he was wearing and he refused to speak up or take off the mask. He was wearing a graphic t-shirt with a leather bomber jacket which they found to be unprofessional considering how serious the matters being discussed were and the cost of the consultation. He seemed frustrated that they wanted general legal advice instead of asking questions; however, none of them have any legal knowledge that would allow them to know what questions they should be asking. When they left, he once more couldn’t be bothered to stand up or shake anyones hand. Seems like he didn’t need their business so they went somewhere with more professionalism and respect.
Competent, kind, prompt, knowledgeable especially senior issues!!!

If I could give a zero I would, due to the fact that Charles Oren lied to me and was of no help for this new pendemic, over the phone hearing . I had to look online for help from other attorneys. On the day of my hearing which was scheduled for 12 p.m. the Judge called me and she informed me that my attorney would not be present so I could reschedule and wait six more months or continue without him.  I chose to continue without him . 15 minutes into the hearing I was getting a call , that I could not answer but when I checked after the hearing, it was from Charles Oren, telling me the hearing was going to be rescheduled because of the judge and that he was sorry which was a lie since I was with the Judge in the hearing
He ended up coming into the hearing and I was asked by the judge if it was okay I said yes, he then begin to argue with the judge about why he was late.  After the hearing he left a message message saying it didn't look good.  Two weeks later when I was approved for benefits I then filed a review of attorney fees with a letter to the judge. It's been 6 months and I was just informed that Charles Oren will not be getting paid so justice has been served but it was a very stressful time for me I wouldn't recommend him.

Things that were done wrong
-I was never given a copy of my file from SS by them. Negative records weren’t identified by him or countered either in the hearing or on appeal.
-Calls are never returned and questions are never answered. I saw him for a total of less than 10 minutes. He cancelled our before hearing meeting.
-The appeal he filed with the appeals council after he lost at the hearing was 7 words long. I fired Oren and the next lawyer’s appeal was 100’s of pages long.
-I received a bench decision at the next hearing because the Oren hearing denial contradicted itself many times. No contradiction was challenged by Oren.
-I hired Matthew Holmberg after this and the difference of aptitude and professionalism was palpable. If he hadn’t fixed Oren’s mistakes I would’ve never even gotten a hearing with the appeals council and would have lost years of back pay.
-Those mistakes will end up costing me over $10K extra in lawyer’s fees. Almost a year of my disability income.
I don't have a bad thing 2 say about this place.
Won't take my husband disability case
I hired Mr Oren for my disability case and he helped me get the money I deserved. Hes a straight shooter and an honest man.

The office is rude without cause and do not return calls. They did not submit medical information needed for my case. They disregarded important questions, always leaving you in the dark. After a couple years of going to Oren I reached the 25% cap. So he advised me to go to another Lawyer. While I did have to start fresh my case was approved in a few months with No fee attached.

Went in today for a consultation and Mr Oren was very Polite and kind he explained everything in which I could understand and decided to take my case. I’m looking forward to his representation  and I am super super happy I have Mr Oren on my side. If you want an amazing Lawyer that knows his stuff and is professional💯💯💯 Oren&Oren Law Is A Must Go Too hands down.
They were very friendly there
Great at what they do.

Had my hearing yesterday and while I don't know the outcome yet, I wouldn't hesitate to recommend Mr. Oren. If you are looking for someone to babysit you and hold your hand the whole way, he isn't going to be for you. If however, you understand that he is not a doctor, a vocational expert, nor a judge, and can only work with what your doctors provide, then you will be just fine.
He is an attorney, he has no control over your disability or health. He can't fabricate evidence or work with what he doesn't have. This is your hearing, you should be the one to make sure he has the most up to date records available. I know it seems a bit strange cause you are thinking, "Then why am I paying him?" The answer is simple, you're paying him for his legal knowledge and experience in SSID hearings, and he won't disappoint.

I wish I could give this attorney's office a zero rating. There's no contact with the attorney, you're forced to talk to the front desk ladies because Mr. Charlie Oren is never in the office. Also, he's only at the Bakersfield location on some Friday's. He doesn't care about the small SSI cases and it's probably because he has too many to keep up with all of them. I called him to express my concerns and how it's been over a year and a half since I went to him with my case, his response "Find a different attorney." give or take a few curse words. Do you really want someone representing you when his response is for you to find someone else?

If you choose to use this law practice do so with the knowledge that you are just a number!!! your calls  are never returned, the staff is placating,rude and at times hostile.
The paperwork is not filed  in a timly matter, which in turn delays an already exhaustive wait..... please do your self a favor don't even consider this office when looking for a disability attorney.

Charles Oren is the reason why I won my case. I appreciate everything he has done for me and the staff is very friendly and always answered my question also try to remember alot of cases take time and you must be patient the results will come just don't try to force it too much and ask questions.

Lucky for me, my friend got 3 tickets in Bakersfield/ Kern County and he got all of them got dismissed, and guess who he used……… Bigger & Harman Law Firm :)

reviews.text:
I recently had an excellent experience with Bigger & Harman and especially attorney Mark Bigger.
A few months ago, I received a lane change ticket on I-5 while driving my truck. Despite explaining my side to the officer, I was still cited. As a professional truck driver, maintaining a clean CDL record is extremely important for my career and livelihood.
After the incident, I contacted Bigger & Harman in Bakersfield, a well-known and respected firm. Caution! If I can give them zero stars, I would! My husband hired Jesse to represent him in a DUI case from when he was a minor, which unexpectedly resurfaced now that he is in his mid-30s. He had no idea there was a warrant for his arrest, as he was able to obtain a Class A license and recently register a firearm without any issues. At no point was he informed that a warrant existed, and he has otherwise maintained a clean record.

After being pulled over and learning about the warrant, my husband immediately contacted Jesse. He was told the case would be easy and not to worry. Trusting this reassurance, my husband made his first payment prior to his initial court date. Shortly after, Jesse began encouraging him to serve jail time, which was confusing given the earlier assurances.

Jesse made no apparent effort to fight the case. Every conversation consisted of advising my husband to take jail time. When my husband had questions or concerns, calls were rarely returned—yet reminders for payment were always prompt. Most disappointing, Jesse failed to appear when it was time for my husband to surrender. Even the judge expressed confusion and stated that my husband’s attorney should have been present.

This was an extremely frustrating and disappointing experience. It felt like a complete waste of money, as my husband could have handled the matter himself without legal representation. If you are looking for an attorney who will actively defend you and communicate effectively, this law firm is not the right choice.

A little over four months ago, our world was turned upside down when our son was detained by law enforcement and taken into custody. The charges he faced carried a sentence of 45 years to life. Immediately my husband began researching local criminal defense attorneys and narrowed it down to three highly regarded options.
After consulting with all three attorneys, it was clear that Tony Lidgett was the right choice. From the moment we engaged with him and his team, they treated us with the utmost respect and compassion, going beyond just handling a case or financial transaction. They treated our son as a person, not merely a defendant. Tony, along with Joey, visited our son regularly. Additionally, Tony, Joey, and Todd were always available to answer any questions or concerns we had throughout the process, regardless of the day of the week. This made us feel like family, knowing we didn’t have to wait until Monday to reach out.
Tony’s approach was truly personal, and his team made us feel more like family than clients. Thanks to their unwavering dedication and expertise, our son will soon be home. We will forever be grateful for Tony and his team’s support and commitment to our family during this challenging time.

Review for GBLA (Greater Bakersfield Legal Assistance):

I’m very disappointed with my experience at GBLA. Every time I’ve called or asked for help, I’ve been dismissed or passed around. I’m a low-income tenant trying to follow the proper legal steps, but instead of receiving clear guidance or compassion, I’ve been told things like “we don’t handle that” with no direction on where to go next.

GBLA’s website and mission say they help with housing and eviction cases, but in reality, it feels like no one wants to take the time to listen or connect me to real resources. When people are scared and trying to do things the right way, we need kindness and clear help — not to be made to feel like a burden.

I truly hope GBLA will review how they communicate with clients in crisis. Even a small amount of understanding and follow-through could make a big difference for those of us trying to stay housed and follow the law.

reviews.text:
“I finally found a great law firm that specializes in Trials.”

I’m a retired U.S. Navy SEAL, living in San Diego, CA. While visiting my family back East for Christmas, I was in a severe car accident.
I was stopped at a red light when a fully loaded 18 wheeler rear-ended me at 55+  miles an hour.

Even though San Diego is the second largest city in California,  I could not find a trial lawyer to represent me, with the standards that I expect from a law firm. I knew there would be a good chance of my case going to trial.  I knew if I had a good law firm that specialized in trial cases, the other side would think twice before going to trial.

Friends and family help me look for a lawyer in California that specializes in trial. A  friend found Rodriguez & Associates in Bakersfield, California over four hours north of San Diego. After researching and talking with them over the phone. I knew this was the firm I wanted to represent me.  Although we never met in person, we were able to  build our case remotely .

We settled before we went to trial. I know the other side didn’t want to take a chance of going to trial with Rodriguez & Associates because of their great reputation.

Thank you.
Kraig Dorner   U.S. Navy SEAL (Ret)
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi, Kraig. Thank you so much for leaving us a stellar review!

reviews.text: Our experience with this law firm had  given us  a calming reassurance that our suffering and pain would be positively vindicated . From the time we consulted with our brilliant Atty Joel Andreesen we had no doubt he would be working for the best outcome to bring justice and closure in a very positive way for us. Our case is just one of the many they have successfully closed but we are deeply grateful for all the behind the scenes hard work that they have been pursuing to end our case positively. We believe this is the firm to consult when you need a positive results oriented law firm.

I went through 3 different case managers, and all 3 of them didn't like to keep me informed on my case. They rarely, and I mean rarely returned my calls. I had to call 2-3 times hoping they weren't busy so I could get an update on my case. Seems like they didn't keep the medical offices that I used updated either. Because the doctors office had called me demanding payment when the case wasn't even close to finishing. It was a stressful time during my accident; but I didn't think most of the stress would come from MY lawyer. I didn't even finish the medical recovery plan the 1st... or was it the 2nd case manager... set for me. I really thought if I was really being scammed, I would have to pay for the medical expenses out of my own pocket. Luckily DK was not a scam, and after almost 2 years I did get my settlement check minus the medical and the lawyer fees 🙄. Even with me receiving my settlement check, I had to call them a couple of times. It was a great relationship for a few months in the beginning, but it all went down hill. Would I recommend them? You probably know the answer to that. If you've made it this far (reading this comment) best of luck to you and I hope you are made whole asap.

Words can't express how thankful I am for Mr. David Faulkner's help. I have made some bad decisions in my past which I have since overcome. Today I went to court on my own, attempting to expunge my record. Well, lo and behold my request was denied simply because I'm not familiar with the law and most big words used in court. As I was leaving an "angel in a suit," Mr. David Faulkner stopped me and said he could help me. He so happened to be there in the courtroom as the Judge was speaking to me, so he knew what was happening about my case. He took me back into the courtroom and spoke to the Judge, Probation Officer, and the District Attorney. He was able to show them their mistakes and how they overlooked some important factors in my paperwork. In the end, the Judge granted my request for expungement and the Probation Officer even apologized to me. Mr. Faulkner fought for me and I wasn't even his client, so just imagine what he does for his clients. This just goes to show you there are still good people out there and Mr. Faulkner is definitely one of them. He came to my rescue even though he did not have to. Not only is he personable, and easy to talk to, but he is a very intelligent individual and really knows what he is doing. I am so grateful for all his help! Thank you Mr. Faulkner.

reviews.text: I have been in and out of the family law court system in Bakersfield over several years and I can tell you that good attorneys are hard to come by! And great paralegals are even harder to come by! It's important you know, that paralegals end up doing much of the work on your case and when you have a truly competent paralegal it makes all the difference! Jonelle Coronado, at the Gorski Firm, is the best of the best! She's smart, responsive, knowledgeable, patient and she sincerely cares about you and your case. Whether it's a divorce or custody issue you're going through, these matters are emotionally draining, anxiety causing and upsetting, Jonelle is very understanding and compassionate. She sincerely wants to help and never makes you feel like you're a nuisance. It makes all the difference in the world to have a paralegal (who you really end up speaking to more often than the attorney) who is empathetic, intelligent and responsive to you during such emotionally difficult times. Jonelle goes above and beyond as a paralegal - you NEED her and you need the Gorski Firm!

reviews.text:
I had an excellent experience working with Attorney George Horgan and his paralegal, Hillary, during a very serious and sensitive matter involving the safety of myself and my daughter.

From the beginning, George and his team were professional, prepared, and persistent. They provided me with everything I needed to build a strong case and took the time to walk me through each step of the process so I understood what to expect. Their attention to detail and knowledge of the law made a significant difference in the outcome of my case.

Hillary was especially responsive and supportive. I had multiple phone calls and meetings with her, and she always answered my questions promptly, professionally, and thoroughly. Communication was consistent, timely, and reassuring throughout the entire process.

Because of their hard work and dedication, I was able to successfully obtain a restraining order, which was critical for the safety and well-being of both me and my child. I truly felt supported and advocated for the entire time.

I am very grateful for the level of care, professionalism, and commitment shown by George Horgan and his team. I would highly recommend this office to anyone needing strong, reliable legal representation especially in cases where safety and protection are a priority.

My name is Brandon Pacheco, and I’m writing this from the perspective of a parent who dealt directly with this attorney as opposing counsel, not as a client.

In my case, this lawyer failed to properly handle critical procedural issues that have had long-lasting consequences for normal parenting. Most notably, there was no Judgment of Parentage submitted, yet custody and visitation were funneled under a Domestic Violence Restraining Order (DVRO) instead of being established through the appropriate parentage and custody framework. That procedural choice created unnecessary restrictions and complications around basic parenting time that should never have existed in the first place.
Whether through oversight or strategy, failing to secure a proper judgment before routing custody through a DVRO made routine co-parenting far more difficult and prolonged conflict that could have been avoided with competent procedural handling.

Experiences like this are exactly why I founded Pacheco Court Prep. Our work exists to help parents—mothers and fathers—who are left dealing with the fallout when attorneys miss procedural fundamentals or fail to protect their client’s long-term parenting rights. We focus on helping self-represented parents understand court structure, filings, and process so they aren’t trapped by mistakes that should never have happened.

I hope this review encourages greater attention to procedural accuracy, especially in family law cases where children and parent-child relationships are directly impacted.

— Brandon Pacheco
Founder, Pacheco Court Prep

Absolutely horrible attorney very rude and condescending to clients his employees can’t communicate they got weeks without getting answers to simple questions they drop the ball and completely forget to do things hey said they would do not go with this attorney under any circumstance

Don't expect customer service, expect delays in your callbacks and emails being answered, and questionable billing practices like being being for a full hour at $500 when they were only on the phone with you for 25 minutes. Take a screenshot of all your calls. I  fired them after being with them for 12 weeks--got new representation and they did more for me in 2 weeks than George did in 3 months.

Mr. Horrigan is great during a consultation.  He knows what he is talking about and even judges ask for his knowledge on certain cases in court, he will let you know that.

My problem was he represented me from September 2016 until January 2024, he dropped me like a hot potato when I didn’t have another retainer fee.  He wanted $6,000 (another retainer fee) in order for him to move forward with my case, as I still had so many loose ends.  It makes sense why people refer to lawyers as sharks.

I had so many continuances throughout those years. I honestly think the attorneys play games and purposely drag things out, more time = more money. If they needed time to prepare for a client’s case that is one thing, but when you return to court a few months later and nothing more gets accomplished, it is very frustrating.

Most of the time Mr. Horrigan would walk right past me in the court hallway and had no clue who I was.  You would think after over 7 years as a client and oodles of money spent, he would recognize me.

Mr. Horrigan is not totally to blame as I had three different paralegals during my time as a client. If he went to court on my behalf and said I didn’t need to be present, I had to call and find out what took place and look up on-line just to find out my next court date.  Often times I had to call or email my paralegal to make sure paperwork was done and ready for the next court date too.

Beware if your paralegal is sick, has a baby, off due to surgery, etc. and is out of the office. I was told no one else could help me and I would have to wait until my paralegal returned.

Had any of my paralegals kept in touch with me and let me know what was going on from time-to-time, that would have saved me a lot of time, money and worry.

Anytime you call or email you are on the clock and they are billing for every minute.  My emails most of the time were not responded to in 24, 48, 72 hours or even two weeks or longer.  Often times I had no choice but to send a follow up email (which cost me more money that I was billed) to find out what was going on.  I preferred emailing so I had things in writing. Over the phone it is a crap shoot she said/client said.  Leaving me with no documentation backing anything up!

He let me know if I wasn’t happy I could go elsewhere.  I should have left then, would have saved myself a ton of money.

Now I am representing myself. It does take a lot of time and energy to figure out everything but I have gotten more done on my own than when I was a client.

Just writing all of this so hopefully you can avoid some of the experiences I had to deal with.  An attorney is only as good as his paralegal!!!

reviews.text:
My wife was severely injured unintentionally by another party.  Once the police report was received we contacted the other party’s insurance company trying to determine how to proceed.  While I was away from home, an insurance adjuster attempted to interrogate my wife on the phone.  My wife was medicated for her injuries, and experiencing post-concussion syndrome (PCS).  She was also sent a large amount of paperwork by them, which would have absolved them of responsibility.
The situation we were in was very stressful, overwhelming, and full of uncertainty.  More importantly I needed to navigate my wife’s personal care needs, and determine the direction of her medical treatment going forward.
We were referred to Matt Clark, Esq. with Chain Cohn Clark.  He immediately relieved us of all of the pressure created by the insurance company, medical billing, etc.  I could then focus my attention on caring for my wife, and navigating her medical care.  Once we retained Mr. Clark everything had to pass through him and his team.  If we had any questions Mr. Clark was available to guide and answer them.
Matt Clark Esq. and his team at Chain Cohn Clark are first class.  Mr. Clark was a God send for us.  The firm is local, which makes them accessible.  If you’re experiencing something that you think that you may need their help, don’t hesitate.

We would like to say Than you very much for all your help through this difficult time in out lives.  Matt, Grace and all the staff were very helpful in helping us with our accident, the paper work, insurance companies and so much more!  We would definitely recommend Chain, Cohn and Clark to anyone in need of a great attorney and staff!!  And one thing I would like to mention is that they are right here in Bakersfield.  They have a caring hometown attitude and work very hard to help their clients!  Thank you so much to the whole team for all your help to make a very difficult situation in our lives a lot more manageable.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your heartfelt message. We’re grateful you trusted our team to help guide you through such a difficult time. It means a lot to hear that our local, hometown approach made a difference for you. We’re proud to serve Bakersfield and to stand beside our clients when they need it most.

reviews.text:
I spent nearly 5 years waiting for Doug Fitz-Simmons to get me a check only to get screwed out of everything because he made one of the most fundamental mistakes a licensed lawyer can make in a personal injury case. I wont go into all the details but I assure you this law firm dropped the ball then is walking away like they never made one. I dont care that they lost $100k plus in expenses and fees. If they would have been thorough in their job I'd be sitting in a lounge chair in a sunny destination boastong about their devotion and dedication to their clients.....but I'm not. I'm angrily making this post so some of you will choose to look elsewhere and not let their lose ends and poor quality control destroy any compensation you deserved. My case was a mid 7-figure case against CalTrans for leaving a 3ft tall x 7ft wide tree stump in a clear recovery zone where there should have been no obsticals whatsoever as stated in the Caltrans design manual......and they lost the case.

Good Luck if you choose them to represent you and I really mean Good Luck!

P.s. if your from outta town lookout. They've even created a video series on how you don't want to go with the out of town team. I should have seen it coming

reviews.text: When my wife was rear-ended last May, totalling her car and another vehicle, the driver responsible fled the scene. Her facial cuts and whiplash required months of treatment, and the reason was senseless. When the driver was identified and apprehended, and found to be insured, my next call was to Tanya Alsheikh at Chain Cohn Clark. I've known her for years and trusted her to see if there was some semblance of compensation my wife could receive. She and her paralegal Grace Jimenez were kind, caring, professional, and so persistent in determining what was available from the responsible driver's insurance. We knew the ultimate settlement could not fully purchase a new car, and my wife continues to experience anxiety in close traffic conditions. However, we could not be happier with the way Tanya and Grace did all the heavy lifting in the process to deliver maximum compensation. Their communication was constant and crystal clear. I have already recommended other accident victims to them, and will continue to do so, with complete confidence and conviction that they will be represented as strongly as possible, just as my wife was. Thank you Tanya and Grace.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for placing your trust in our team during such a challenging time. We’re truly grateful for the opportunity to help your family seek justice and support your wife’s recovery. And your confidence and referrals mean so much to us.

reviews.text:
Look no further than Chain | Cohn | Clark in Bakersfield.
As someone who works closely with victims of DUI crashes, I can tell you that this firm isn’t just legal representation — they are a lifeline. For over 12 years, they’ve been a committed sponsor of Mothers Against Drunk Driving (MADD) Kern County, helping us serve families through some of their darkest moments. Without their continued support, we simply wouldn’t be able to help as many victims and families as we do today.
On a deeply personal note — when my own son was killed, Chain Cohn Clark didn’t just offer legal help. They made sure my grandson would grow up safe and secure. That kind of compassion can’t be taught — it’s part of who they are.
This is a civil law firm that stands with the community. They support victims, give back generously, and follow through with heart. If you need a law firm that puts people first — this is the one.
Carla Pearson
Senior, Victim Advocate
For Mothers Against Drunk Driving
I have been advocating for families for 23 years -
reviews.ownerResponse: Carla, thank you from the bottom of our hearts. Your words mean more than we can express, and we are truly honored to stand beside you and MADD Kern County in this life-saving work. Your strength, advocacy, and heart inspire us every day. We are especially grateful for the trust you’ve placed in our firm, not only professionally but personally. Supporting victims and families is part of our mission and it’s a responsibility we carry with deep compassion, thanks to leaders like you.

reviews.text:
As the committee chair for VillageFest, one of Bakersfield's top annual events, I'm proud of what we've accomplished. This popular beer and wine tasting, food, and music event donates 100% of profits to our foundation, CAREforkids.org, benefiting local children's charities.

This year, Chain Cohn Clark sponsored safe rides home with Lyft, providing $30 vouchers used by over 110 attendees, totaling $3,300 in support. It's a great way to enhance safety while enjoying the festival!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the kind words and for the incredible work you do through Village Fest and CARE. We’re proud to support an event that brings our community together while giving back in such a meaningful way. Providing safe rides home is just one small part of keeping the celebration fun and responsible, and we’re honored to be a part of it!

reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Exceptional Legal Support During My Most Difficult Time

I can’t thank Doug Fitz-Simmons, Chain Chin Clark, and their incredible law team enough for the unwavering support they provided throughout my car accident settlement. From the very beginning, they treated me not just as a client, but as a person going through one of the most vulnerable chapters of my life.

Doug and Chain were consistently honest, compassionate, and incredibly thorough. They took the time to explain every step of the process, always making sure I felt informed and empowered. Their professionalism was matched only by their genuine care—checking in on my well-being, listening without judgment, and advocating fiercely on my behalf.

Thanks to their expertise and dedication, I was able to secure a settlement that truly reflected the extent of my injuries and losses. It wasn’t just about the financial outcome—it was about feeling seen, heard, and supported every step of the way.

If you’re looking for a legal team that combines integrity, empathy, and results, I wholeheartedly recommend Doug Fitz-Simmons, Chain Chin Clark, and their team. They helped me reclaim peace of mind when I needed it most.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your deeply thoughtful words. We're truly honored to have supported you through such a difficult time. Knowing you felt seen, heard, and supported means everything to us. Wishing you continued healing and peace.

reviews.text: Maison law immigration office is the best immigration office in Bakersfield. I strongly recommend it. My brother was a Legal Permanent Resident (green card holder), and unfortunately, he encountered criminal charges and got his green card revoked, which led to deportation. After going to different lawyers outside Bakersfield, no one gave us hope. We got various responses, and some even told us his deportation was for life. Fortunately, we found Maison Law’s office, which gave us hope and explained the process of his case thoroughly. The attorney, Rozanna Gasparian, Legal assistant Sylvia, Paralegal Sissi, and all the staff helped tremendously with his case. All his paperwork was submitted promptly, from getting his charges dismissed to appearing before an immigration judge in court and submitting his renewal of I-90. Also, very helpful in answering any questions, doubts, or concerns. My brother is now again a Legal Permanent Resident (green holder) and is back in the USA. We are just incredibly thrilled to have him back, and after so many years, it was worth the wait. We thank God for leading us to such an incredible immigration office. We will forever be thankful for the hard work and dedication the office put into my brother’s case. I highly, highly recommend it. Their work ethic is tremendously impeccable.

I was suggested to get a new set of friends like if he knew somewhat more than what I was sharing with him but sided for the others instead of defending me. I would have given less than 1 star if offered, for one when he was first hired I was incarcerated and he never came to see me he sent an investigator. He was a no show for my first court appearance. Also, he never wanted me to appear in court, stating that he would appear for me, which is legal but I wanted to know what was going on with my case. It was wise for me to appear at every hearing because he was working against me  I have a record and he couldn’t move pass that being an ex district attorney even though I confessed my innocence. I provided him evidence and he claimed he lost it. Mr. kyle Humphrey kept telling me to get in touch with his investigator and when I did he didn’t know what I was talking about. I informed him that I wanted a speedy trial and he misinformed me on what they could do to me without a witness or a victim just on a statement that was made. After fully paying him to represent me I had to go outside of his firm for accurate information. He tried to have me pay 80k for a trial and said he needed 40 k down I went with someone else and they got the charges dropped in 2 court sessions and Mr. Lyle Humphrey had me convinced that if I didn’t agree to everything he wanted me to do that I was facing life. My opinion of Mr. Kyle Humphrey is if you don’t have a clean record he can’t or will not be willing to help you so keep your money in your pocket.
professional and confident
